SDKFAtIb.intro: This is the Hungarian Dragon. An opening first found by Rapport—a player well-known for his aggressive and creative play—the Hungarian is a unique approach to the Sicilian. Preventing many of white's h4-g4 ideas that are so often our headaches as Dragon players, I definitely prefer this line over the normal SD. The main idea of your position is to trade off the dark-squared bishops through Nxd4, followed by Bh6. Even though this seems counterintuitive at first, it ends up reducing much of the…

W20fAyaV.intro: The KIA setup can be aggressive and/or positional and can go multiple different ways based on how the opponent plays. In the KIA, we normally fianchetto the kingside and (most of the time) queenside bishop. Nf3 is where the king's knight always goes, and the queen's knight almost always goes on d2 (To defend/go to c4 & to defend e4). With this setup, we play for c4 and eventually, potentially e4. (d4 is played very early) Finally, castle kingside. The arrows showcase different moves/ideas…

Wwz6xMZk.intro: Hello and welcome to my new study on how to Burn the Kan & Taimanov Sicilians! In this study, we'll see how to defeat Black when we play against these 2 Sicilians. This is the 4th study in a series of studies on the Open Sicilian for White. The others are linked below: Burn the Dragon, Kalashnikov, and Pin Sicilians!: https://lichess.org/study/3VYZAzSb Burn the Najdorf!: https://lichess.org/study/KvhZf1BF Burn the Sveshnikov, Four Knights, and Scheveningen!: https://lichess.org/study/eVEnm3i4

XCYnNukC.intro: This is known as the fantasy variation, which occurs in the caro-kann defense. Many players, including me, *hate* playing against this. And that's why, I have created this study. I have learned a secret weapon against the fantasy and I'm going to share it with you.

Y3o9RfzG.intro: This is the 4th game of the 1971 candidates match between Bobby Fischer and Mark Taimanov. This endgame is considered one of the classics in chess history.

m2ovNEWF.intro: English opening is defined by 1.c4. It is semi closed or often called "flank" opening which can lead to many semi-closed positions such as Kings Indian, Queens Indian, Ben-Oni or some semi-open positions if black decides to counter it with 1. .. e5 which can often lead to reverse sicilian defenses. The most usefull characteristic of English is that it can transpose to a lot of different openings which are highly dependable on blacks first move. Black can proceed with ( 3 most commonly played )…

t3kJNxRD.intro: Originally, this study was intended as a small rep for a close friend of mine against d4. The last move is his weakness, as the statistics speaks in favor of White. The queenside pawn debut implies closed or semi-closed positions, in which the game develops in a positional and calm manner. From the first moves White limits Black in development, there is no Sicilian Defense or Caro-Kann. However, we can make White nervous or surprised and dictate our terms from the first move.

t4pxmBLs.intro: Hey, you there. Have you ever thought about when this beautiful, intellectually challenging, brutal, bloody...well, when modern chess was created? Where did it all start? So buckle up your seatbelts and get into the time machine so I can start the engines.
